macro_segments.title: Why Israel went to Babylon: Historical and Spiritual Reasons

micro_segments.title: Ulla's teaching: Abundance of dates and ease of livelihood enabled Torah study
Anecdote: Ulla's stomach ache from overeating dates; comparing Babylonian devotion to Torah despite material ease
Discussion of Berditchever story and lesson about material comfort not ensuring spiritual growth

macro_segments.title: Theological significance of the Beis HaMikdash as a concentrated place of Divine presence

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar's exegesis: Why the nations call it the God of Yaakov, not Avraham or Yitzchak
Rabbi Yochanan: Gathering of exiles as great as creation of heaven and earth
Extended teaching on the significance of bayis (house) versus har (mountain) and sadeh (field); the power of constructed sacred space

macro_segments.title: Membership in the Pesach group: household members versus independent participants

micro_segments.title: Orphan with two guardians: he can choose which slaughtered Pesach to eat
Baraita: Who can be slaughtered for without consent—minors and Canaanite slaves; who needs consent—adults, Jewish slaves, wife
Discussion of household as objective category versus individual consent; wife's independence and ability to opt out
Gemara's clarification: "without their knowledge" means without explicit objection, but buy-in is assumed

macro_segments.title: Slave of two partners: when conflict prevents participation

micro_segments.title: Dispute between Rav Eina Saba and Rav Nachman: can slave of two partners eat from either's Pesach
Resolution: depends on whether partners are friendly or hostile; if hostile, neither can claim the slave

macro_segments.title: Half-slave, half-free person: liminal status and forced manumission

micro_segments.title: Mishnaic debate between Beit Shammai and Beit Hillel on the case of half-slave, half-free
Beit Shammai's position: forced manumission for sake of world population; master's control ends though status remains half-half
Beit Hillel accepts Beit Shammai's view; contemporary application to agunah (women in marital limbo)

macro_segments.title: Master tells slave to slaughter Pesach: uncertainty about which animal is designated

micro_segments.title: Baraita: if slave slaughters goat or lamb without specification, master eats from whichever regardless of preference
If slave slaughters both: normally only first one is valid, but complicated by question of prior designation
If slave forgot master's instruction: makes stipulation that one animal is his, one is master's; can designate after slaughter
Both master and slave forgot: neither knows whose is whose; both Pesachs valid but exempt from Pesach Sheni

macro_segments.title: Anecdote of King and Queen: demonstrating that designation matters only when owner has preferences

micro_segments.title: Story: King and Queen told servants to slaughter Pesach; servants shechted both; they go to Rabban Gamliel for ruling
Rabban Gamliel's answer: since king and queen don't care about food preferences, they eat from the first one
Second story: lizard found in royal kitchen; Rabban Gamliel determines it was alive, meal remains pure
Narrative conclusion: king dependent on queen; queen dependent on Rabban Gamliel; all sustained by wisdom of Sages

macro_segments.title: Forgotten designation before blood-throwing: Pesach Sheni obligation

micro_segments.title: Abaye: if master forgot which animal was designated before zerika (blood-throwing), Pesach Sheni is required
Reasoning: at zerika, both must be fit for eating and designated; if unknown at that moment, obligation triggered

macro_segments.title: Mixed skins of five Pesachs with blemish on one: exemption from Pesach Sheni despite one being invalid

micro_segments.title: Case: five people's Pesach skins mixed; blemish found on one; all burned
Exemption from Pesach Sheni because impossible to identify obligated person; solutions attempted and rejected
Difficulty: how to fulfill obligation when one person is obligated but unknown; potential solutions with stipulations

topical_tags.term: Minors and slaves under parental/master control
Zachin (doing favor for someone without consent)
Half-slave, half-free (Mechetzah eved, mechetzah ben chorin)
Nashim da'atan kallah (Women's minds are light/easily satisfied)
